findings.detail: Description of "catalyst_send_lite_event" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

description:
List all available pre-built compliance framework packs.

        Returns each pack (GDPR, SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, EU AI Act,
        Bribery Act, AML/KYC) with its name, description, rule count, and
        whether it is currently active for this organisation.
        Activate a pack with

description.name: catalyst_activate_compliance_framework
description.description:
Activate a pre-built compliance framework pack for this organisation.

        framework: one of gdpr, soc2, iso27001, hipaa, pci_dss, eu_ai_act,
        bribery_act, aml_kyc.
        Once activated, the pack's DENY/REQUIRE/ADVISE rules are merged into
        every subsequent compliance scan — no d

description.name: catalyst_list_compliance_rules
description.description:
List all compliance rules currently in effect for the organisation.

        Returns rules from three sources combined and deduplicated:
        - Rules extracted from uploaded policy documents
        - Custom org-level rules (PATCH /compliance/rules)
        - Rules from any activated framework pa

description.name: catalyst_register_machine_actor
description.description:
Register a machine actor (agent, automation) with Catalyst governance.

        runtime_type: zapier_zap | n8n_workflow | openai_assistant | make_scenario |
                      claude_agent | custom_agent | internal_worker.
        governance_mode: observe | advisory | proposal | strict.
        r

description.name: catalyst_evaluate_agent_action
description.description:
Evaluate whether an agent action is permitted by the governance gate.

        Returns decision: 'allow' | 'proposal_required' | 'deny'.
        High-risk actors with allow decisions are escalated to proposal_required.
        The actor must be registered and active for this tenant.

description.name: catalyst_register_workflow
description.description:
Register an AI workflow for EU AI Act compliance governance.

        risk_level: unacceptable (rejected) | high | limited | minimal.
        mode: observe | advisory | proposal. High-risk workflows are forced to proposal.
        Sets ai_standards=True to opt into the Built to AI Standards evidence

description.name: catalyst_send_lite_event
description.description:
Send a lifecycle event for a registered AI workflow.

        event_type examples: run.started, action.executed, approval.requested,
        run.completed, run.failed. Idempotent via request_id (UUID recommended).
        Payload is scrubbed of credentials before storage.

description.name: catalyst_get_workflow_trust
description.description:
Get the four-component trust score for a registered AI workflow.

        Score = 0.25×ai_declared + 0.25×risk_eval + 0.25×override + 0.25×(1−failure).
        Range 0.0–1.0. window_days controls the lookback period (1–365).

description.name: catalyst_export_workflow_audit
description.description:
Export audit events for an AI workflow as JSON.

        since: ISO timestamp filter (inclusive). ai_standards_only: limit to
        events from workflows opted into Built to AI Standards.
        Suitable for regulatory submissions and investor due diligence.

description.name: catalyst_weekly_summary
description.description:
Get a 7-day activity rollup: ingested, accepted, declined proposals,
        domain breakdown, priority breakdown, and average risk score.
        Fully deterministic — no LLM calls.

description.name: catalyst_export_ledger
description.description:
Export the immutable SHA-256 chained audit ledger.

        since: ISO timestamp to filter entries (inclusive).
        format: 'json' returns full entries; 'csv' sets entries to null (use REST API for CSV).
        The ledger is tamper-evident — any modification breaks the hash chain.

description.name: catalyst_my_governance
description.description:
Show your governance profile, actor registration, and plan capabilities.

        Returns governance_mode (observe/advisory/proposal/strict), risk_class,
        declared capabilities, and actor_id. Auto-registers you in observe mode
        on first connection — the lightest governance touch.

description.name: catalyst_check_action
description.description:
Check if a capability/action is permitted by your governance policy.

        Returns decision: 'allow' | 'proposal_required' | 'deny' with guidance.
        Call BEFORE any consequential action (API call, data write, send, deploy).

        If title is provided and decision is 'proposal_required',

description.name: catalyst_log_task
description.description:
Log a task or action into the Catalyst governance pipeline.

        Records work, decisions, and actions for audit and governance.
        priority: Low | Medium | High | Critical.
        domain: security | product | operations | compliance | finance | legal | other.
        Returns ingested count

description.name: catalyst_pending_approvals
description.description:
List proposals currently awaiting governance approval.

        Shows proposals with status pending or in review. Use this to check whether
        an action you submitted is still waiting for human sign-off before you proceed.
        Returns count and full proposal objects with IDs for catalyst_aw

description.name: catalyst_await_approval
description.description:
Check approval status for a specific proposal.

        Returns approved: true/false and clear guidance on whether to proceed.
        Call this after catalyst_check_action returns 'proposal_required'.
        Do NOT proceed with the gated action until approved: true is returned.

        POLLING vs

description.name: catalyst_register_approval_webhook
description.description:
Register a callback URL to receive proposal approval notifications.

        Subscribes callback_url to proposal.accepted and proposal.declined events.
        When a human approves or declines any proposal, Catalyst POSTs the event
        to your URL with the resolved proposal IDs in data.ids.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: catalyst_list_proposals, catalyst_accept_proposals, catalyst_pending_approvals, catalyst_register_approval_webhook · Private data: catalyst_run_compliance_scan, catalyst_activate_compliance_framework, catalyst_list_compliance_rules, catalyst_send_lite_event, catalyst_log_task · Public sink: catalyst_activate_compliance_framework, catalyst_send_lite_event, catalyst_check_action, catalyst_await_approval, catalyst_register_approval_webhook · Destructive: catalyst_run_compliance_scan, catalyst_send_lite_event, catalyst_export_ledger.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: catalyst_list_proposals, catalyst_accept_proposals, catalyst_pending_approvals, catalyst_register_approval_webhook · Private data: catalyst_run_compliance_scan, catalyst_activate_compliance_framework, catalyst_list_compliance_rules, catalyst_send_lite_event, catalyst_log_task · Public sink: catalyst_activate_compliance_framework, catalyst_send_lite_event, catalyst_check_action, catalyst_await_approval, catalyst_register_approval_webhook · Destructive: catalyst_run_compliance_scan, catalyst_send_lite_event, catalyst_export_ledger

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
